action.reasoning: Observation: Best test accuracy 0.7636 from iter 5 (ConvNeXt + GCE + Mixup + SWA), but ViT attempt gave 0.6731. The ConvNeXt checkpoint that achieved 0.7636 is unknown; later runs overwrote best_model.pt etc. Some epoch/swa files (21-50) likely remain from ConvNeXt runs. Need to locate the best ConvNeXt checkpoint to try surpass 0.7636. Analysis: We can evaluate all checkpoints on noisy validation set (val.pt) to find the one with highest accuracy; while noisy val accuracy may not perfectly correlate with clean test, it is a reasonable proxy for models trained with GCE. This evaluation will be fast (~2-3 min). Decision: Write a diagnostic script to load each .pt, detect backbone, compute noisy val accuracy, and output top-5 candidates. Then submit eval for the checkpoint with highest noisy val accuracy. Use current predict.py (center-crop, no TTA) to avoid TTA confusion.
